Key Takeaways:
o Warming your list
o Send them good content
▪ Give them awesome free stuff
▪ 2-3 pieces of content is perfect, that’s a big win
▪ If you let the list go cold, give them a reason why
▪ Don’t be afraid to be personal
▪ Be aspirational
• Tell them a story of someone who achieved something in a
relevant way
o Shot Across the Bow - let them know something is coming
o Pre-Prelaunch assets and planning
o Your current assets - as they relate to Pre-Prelaunch to create buzz, gather
data, build relationship

▪ Raving fans

Pre-Prelaunch Summary
o This is very powerful stuff - use it!
o People outside of PLF don’t know this exists
o People think they can reverse engineer launches but they miss big chunks of the
process - which you now have!
o This is how you get the story out there and get people anticipating your launch
o You’re getting them excited before they even feel like they’re getting pitched
o You should absolutely put this process to work for you in your launch

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Next is warming your list.
We've covered quite a bit when we talk about the shot across the bow. This is huge for
warming the list. You saw how some e-mails I sent were all about being likable, they give
content. Already a lot of that going is on, this isn't that complicated. If you want to warm
your list, you give them good free stuff. That always works.
No matter how cold the list generally is (and trust me everyone does it at one time or
another and probably repeatedly), you send them two or three pieces of good content. The
other thing you do is come back and give them some reason why, and say "Hey I'm sorry,
whatever the reason... I forgot my logins credentials" obviously I'm joking around there.
Just say "Hey I got busy, I had some personal things come up" whatever it is, give
them a reason why and then say, I'm really sorry for not having sent you anything but I've
got some really awesome for you. Go take a look at this special report or this special video,
give them some content.
Don't be afraid to be personal, especially in that apology, if you give them the reason
you've let the list go cold it will make a difference. Coming up on Prelaunch, it's often good to
give a little bit of extra content and down here I've got to be aspirational. Even if you just tell
them a story of someone who achieved something in market or nature, if it's playing guitar,
or carving wooden dolls or growing tomatoes.
You can tell a story of how someone succeeded, especially if they succeeded after they
struggled. People love to have someone to cheer on, someone that went through some
struggles, that's very easy to be aspirational.
We got the shot across the bow, its wonderful for creating Prelaunch warmth right
before the Prelaunch.
Now let's talk about your launch. Let's take a look at current assets. Earlier in this
course, we went through sort of an asset inventory for your entire launch. Now, we're going
to look at current assets as they relate to Pre-Prelaunch.
Things you can think about that you might be able to exploit in your Pre-Prelaunch to
get things cranking, to get that little buzz going, to warm the relationship with your list, to
gather data and your offer or to get a data and objections or gather data in possible launch
story. Everything we talked about in the Prelaunch. These are potential assets that could
help you.
You have your prospect list, your client or customer list, maybe you don't have list yet
but if you do those are incredibly powerful for your Pre-Prelaunch. You may have raving
fans; they could be fans for your products or they could be fans for you. Lot of times, when
you're starting out it's easier to have people who are fans of you, rather than your products.
Especially if you haven't put out any products yet.
You might have a blog, you could have JV's and affiliates, you saw how I used my JV's
and got them all excited. Social media presence, YouTube, Facebook, Twitter any others that

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
can be really powerful for creating the initial buzz. I've often used Twitter and Facebook to
drop little hands about things that are coming.
Forums, preferably forums we have a presence, you don't want to just all the sudden
go into a forum that no one knows you and start posting stuff about your business. That's
spamming the forum, you don't want to do that. We're talking about forums where you have
a presence.
Relationships with mavens and connectors and persons of influence. Relationships
with top blogs, this is where you could possibly be a guest blogger at a blog, or even if it's a
blog where you frequently make comments and you're known on that blog. Speaking at
events, if you can get on stage in an event it can possibly tie in to your Prelaunch.
Teleseminars or webinars, or other places where you might get interviewed. These
are some of the assets that you might have, that could be exploited in a Prelaunch.
Let's go to the objectives now. First of all, you may have to go back and review what's
your launch objective. What do you want from your launch?
Before you can figure out what your Pre-Prelaunch objective is, you have to figure out
what the bigger picture is.
I think it's really important to get the bigger picture and start to drill down when
you're involved in one of this launches. Pre-Prelaunch objective could be: identify the offer,
identify objections, identify the launch story, could be gather testimonial. You saw how I did
that with the testimonial cruncher.
Build buzz, warm the relationship with your list, get JV and affiliates excited to
promote. With almost any Pre-Prelaunch you definitely want to build buzz, and you want to
warm the relationship with your list. Those are probably the two most important.
After identifying your offer, the objection is the real key. Often, when you identify the
offer, you'll surface those objections.
You'll find out what the objections are, probably just going through this process a lot
of times you do that. Other things to think about when you're looking at your Pre-Prelaunch
objective is, who do you want talking about your launch? Hopefully it's the person of
influence, the people that can drive you some traffic, and people that will be open to your
launch and will be excited. Usually those connectors, those mavens, and the persons of
influence.
Finally, who do you want buying your product, and how do you reach them, where do
they hang out? If you're going to generate some Pre-Prelaunch buzz you don't want a bunch
of people that will never buy your product that aren’t in your target market.
These are just things to think about as we start to put together the Pre-Prelaunch
plan. As I mentioned in the intro, this is really powerful stuff. People outside of Product
Launch Formula don't even know this stuff exist. They think they can look at a launch and
reverse engineer what's happening. But they completely miss big, huge chunks of the process
including the Pre-Prelaunch.
This is incredibly powerful, it's how you get the story out there, and get the people
interested in your story, interested in the launch story, interested in the launch, before he
even go live with your Prelaunch.
